Version
  • API Reference
  • Grid
  • Grid


Grid

The grid defines the spatial discretisation and pre-computes all wavenumber operators needed by the solvers.

Grid Types

TypeDimensionsFields
KWaveGrid1D1DNx, dx, x_vec, kx_vec, k
KWaveGrid2D2DNx/Ny, dx/dy, x/y_vec, kx/ky_vec, k
KWaveGrid3D3DNx/Ny/Nz, dx/dy/dz, x/y/z_vec, kx/ky/kz_vec, k

All grid types share mutable time fields dt, Nt, t_array (set by make_time!).

Public Fields (all grid types)

FieldTypeDescription
Nx, Ny, NzIntGrid point counts per dimension
dx, dy, dzFloat64Grid spacing [m]
x_vec, y_vec, z_vecVector{Float64}Centred spatial coordinate vectors [m]
kx_vec, ky_vec, kz_vecVector{Float64}Wavenumber vectors [rad/m]
kArray{Float64}Wavenumber magnitude (shape matches grid)
dtRef{Float64}Time step [s] (set by make_time!)
NtRef{Int}Number of time steps (set by make_time!)
t_arrayVector{Float64}Time vector [s] (set by make_time!)
KWave.AbstractKWaveGrid — Type
AbstractKWaveGrid

Abstract supertype for all k-Wave computational grids.

source
KWave.KWaveGrid1D — Type
KWaveGrid1D <: AbstractKWaveGrid

1D computational grid for k-space simulations.

source
KWave.KWaveGrid2D — Type
KWaveGrid2D <: AbstractKWaveGrid

2D computational grid for k-space simulations.

source
KWave.KWaveGrid3D — Type
KWaveGrid3D <: AbstractKWaveGrid

3D computational grid for k-space simulations.

source
KWave.KWaveGrid — Function
KWaveGrid(Nx, dx) -> KWaveGrid1D
KWaveGrid(Nx, dx, Ny, dy) -> KWaveGrid2D
KWaveGrid(Nx, dx, Ny, dy, Nz, dz) -> KWaveGrid3D

Create a k-Wave computational grid.

Arguments

  • Nx, Ny, Nz: Number of grid points in each dimension
  • dx, dy, dz: Grid spacing in each dimension [m]

Returns

Returns KWaveGrid1D, KWaveGrid2D, or KWaveGrid3D based on the number of arguments. Call make_time! on the result before running a solver.

Example

kgrid = KWaveGrid(256, 0.1e-3, 256, 0.1e-3)
make_time!(kgrid, 1500.0)

See Also

make_time!, k_max, total_grid_points, get_optimal_pml_size

source
KWave.make_time! — Function
make_time!(grid, sound_speed; cfl=0.3, t_end=nothing)

Compute the time array for the simulation based on the CFL stability condition.

Arguments

  • grid: KWaveGrid (any dimension)
  • sound_speed: Maximum sound speed in the medium [m/s] (scalar or array)
  • cfl: CFL number (default: 0.3, must be ≤ 1 for stability)
  • t_end: End time [s] (if nothing, computed from grid diagonal / sound speed)

Notes

Modifies grid.dt[], grid.Nt[], and grid.t_array in-place. Pass maximum(medium.sound_speed) as sound_speed for heterogeneous media.

See Also

KWaveGrid, kspace_first_order, k_max

source
KWave.total_grid_points — Function
total_grid_points(grid)

Return the total number of grid points.

source
KWave.grid_size — Function
grid_size(grid)

Return a tuple of the grid dimensions.

source
KWave.grid_spacing — Function
grid_spacing(grid)

Return a tuple of the grid spacings.

source
KWave.k_max — Function
k_max(grid)

Return the maximum supported wavenumber (Nyquist limit) [rad/m].

k_max = π / dx (for the finest-spaced dimension). Used internally for stability checks; also indicates the highest spatial frequency resolvable on the grid.

See Also

KWaveGrid, make_time!, total_grid_points

source
